title: Motor Triac Short Circuit
shortDescription: The motor triac on the main control board has short-circuited, preventing proper motor control.
fullDescription: Error F01 indicates a short circuit in the motor triac on the electronic control board. The triac regulates power to the wash motor. When it fails, the motor may run uncontrolled or not start at all. Indesit shares this error code platform with Ariston and Indesit machines.

causes.cause: Motor triac on the control board has failed
Short circuit in the motor winding
Damaged wiring between control board and motor
Power surge damage to control board

symptoms: Motor does not start at all
Burning smell from control board area

fixSteps.title: Power reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the washer from mains power for 10 minutes. Reconnect and attempt a new cycle. If F01 returns, further diagnosis is needed.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel and check all wiring connections between the motor and the main control board. Look for burnt, loose, or damaged connectors.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor connector from the PCB. Measure resistance across motor windings (typically 2-8 ohms). Check for short to ground between windings and motor housing.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If motor tests good but F01 persists, the triac on the main PCB has failed. Replace the entire control board module matching your model number.

whenToCallPro: Control board replacement requires precise connector matching and may need reprogramming. Professional service is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F01 motor triac error fix

faq.q: Is Indesit F01 the same as Ariston F01?
faq.a: Yes, Indesit, Hotpoint, and Ariston share the same electronic platform. F01 means the same motor triac fault across both brands.

faq.q: Will a power surge cause F01?
faq.a: Yes, voltage spikes can damage the triac. Using a surge protector is recommended for protection.

faq.q: Can the triac be replaced without replacing the whole board?
faq.a: Technically possible with electronics soldering skills, but full board replacement is more reliable and recommended.

title: Motor Tacho Circuit Fault
shortDescription: The motor tachometer signal is not being received by the control board.
fullDescription: Error F02 indicates the control board is not receiving a speed signal from the motor tachometer. The tacho reports rotation speed to the control board. Without this feedback, the board cannot regulate drum speed safely. Worn carbon brushes are the most common cause on Indesit machines.

causes.cause: Motor carbon brushes are worn down
Tachometer coil on motor has failed
Wiring from tachometer to control board is broken

symptoms: Motor hums but does not turn
Washer stops during wash or spin cycle

fixSteps.title: Reset and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 10 minutes. Plug back in and run a test cycle. If F02 persists, proceed to check the motor.

fixSteps.title: Check motor carbon brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel and locate the motor. Extract the carbon brushes from either side of the motor. If shorter than 1 cm, they need replacement. This is the most common fix for F02.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the tachometer
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the tacho sensor on the rear of the motor. Check that the magnet is secure and the coil is intact. Measure resistance across the tacho coil — expect 100-200 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Replace worn parts
fixSteps.instruction: Replace carbon brushes or tachometer as needed. Carbon brushes are an inexpensive and common DIY repair on Indesit washers.

whenToCallPro: If carbon brushes and tachometer test fine, the control board input circuit may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F02 carbon brushes replacement

faq.q: How often do carbon brushes need replacing on a Hotpoint?
faq.a: Typically every 5-8 years. Heavy use with frequent high-spin cycles accelerates wear.

faq.q: Can I replace carbon brushes myself?
faq.a: Yes, this is one of the most common DIY washing machine repairs. The brushes slide out from either side of the motor housing.

faq.q: How do I know if the brushes are worn?
faq.a: If the brush length is less than 1 cm (about the width of a pencil), they need replacement. You may also see black carbon dust around the motor.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The wash temperature sensor (NTC thermistor) is providing an out-of-range or open circuit reading.
fullDescription: Error F03 indicates the NTC thermistor is reading outside its expected range or has an open circuit. The NTC monitors water temperature so the control board can regulate the heater. Without a valid reading, the washer cannot heat water safely.

causes.cause: Wiring between NTC and control board is broken
NTC connector is corroded or loose
Control board NTC input circuit has failed

symptoms: Water does not heat during wash
Clothes come out cold on hot wash
Cycle runs much longer than expected

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry. Temperature sensor faults can sometimes clear with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the NTC in the heater element housing at the bottom of the tub. Disconnect and measure resistance — approximately 20,000 ohms at room temperature (20°C). Infinity or zero indicates failure.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring and connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the two-wire connection from the NTC to the control board for broken wires, corroded pins, or loose connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the old sensor from the heater housing and insert the new one. Reconnect wiring and run a hot wash to verify.

whenToCallPro: Only if NTC and wiring test good, suggesting a control board fault.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F03 NTC temperature sensor fix

faq.q: What is an NTC sensor?
faq.a: An NTC thermistor is a resistor whose resistance decreases as temperature increases, allowing precise water temperature measurement.

faq.q: Can I wash with F03?
faq.a: The washer may run but will not heat water. Cold washing is possible but not ideal for hygiene or stain removal.

faq.q: Is the NTC inside the heating element?
faq.a: On most Indesit models, the NTC is a separate probe in the heater housing, making it easy to replace independently.

title: Pressure Switch Stuck on Empty
shortDescription: The pressure switch remains in the empty position, causing the machine to overfill.
fullDescription: Error F04 indicates the pressure switch stays in the empty state after the fill valves have been open long enough to fill the tub. This can lead to overfilling. Usually caused by a blocked or disconnected pressure hose.

causes.cause: Pressure switch hose is blocked, kinked, or disconnected
Pressure switch chamber clogged with detergent residue
Control board pressure switch circuit fault

symptoms: Cycle does not progress past fill

fixSteps.title: Check the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel and locate the rubber hose from the tub to the pressure switch. Ensure it is not kinked, cracked, or disconnected. Blow gently through it to check for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Clean the pressure chamber
fixSteps.instruction: Where the hose connects to the tub, clean any detergent buildup from the air chamber using warm water.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the switch and blow into the hose port. You should hear a click when pressure is applied and another when released. No click means the switch is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the connector and hose, remove the mounting clip, and install the new switch.

whenToCallPro: If both hose and switch are fine, the control board may have a faulty input.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F04 pressure switch error fix

faq.q: Can F04 cause flooding?
faq.a: Potentially yes. The machine may overfill. Most machines have secondary overflow protection, but address F04 promptly.

faq.q: What is a pressure switch?
faq.a: It measures water level by sensing air pressure in a tube connected to the tub. As water rises, it compresses air and triggers the switch.

faq.q: How often should I clean the pressure chamber?
faq.a: Annually, especially if you use powder detergent.

title: Drain Pump Fault
shortDescription: The drain pump is not operating correctly or the pressure switch is stuck in the full position.
fullDescription: Error F05 indicates the pressure switch remains in the full state after draining. The drain may be blocked, the pump may have failed, or the pressure switch is stuck. Water remains in the drum.

causes.cause: Drain pump filter clogged with debris
Drain pump impeller jammed or motor failed
Pressure switch stuck in full position

symptoms: Drain pump runs but water does not empty

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the small access panel at the bottom-front. Place towels and a tray below. Slowly unscrew the filter cap and remove debris, coins, buttons, or lint.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose for kinks. Remove from the standpipe and check for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: With filter removed, check if the impeller spins freely. If jammed, clear it. If free but pump does not run, test for voltage during drain.

fixSteps.title: Replace the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump motor has failed, disconnect and replace it.

whenToCallPro: If pump, hose, and filter are all clear but F05 persists.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F05 drain pump blocked fix

faq.q: How do I drain the water manually?
faq.a: Open the filter panel at the bottom-front, place towels and a container below, and slowly unscrew the filter cap.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: Every 1-2 months is recommended.

faq.q: Can a blocked standpipe cause F05?
faq.a: Yes, if the wall drain is blocked, water backs up into the machine.

title: Door Lock / Button Fault
shortDescription: The door interlock mechanism or user interface buttons have a fault.
fullDescription: Error F06 covers faults with the door lock (interlock) or the push-button panel. On some Indesit models it specifically relates to the door lock solenoid; on others it signals a button circuit fault. The machine will not start or stops mid-cycle.

causes.cause: Control panel button stuck or shorted

symptoms: Door appears locked but machine will not start
Machine stops and door will not open

fixSteps.title: Check the door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the door catch and striker. Ensure the door closes firmly and engages fully.

fixSteps.title: Test the door interlock
fixSteps.instruction: Access the interlock from inside the door rim. Disconnect and test continuity across terminals with door closed.

fixSteps.title: Inspect button panel
fixSteps.instruction: If door lock tests good, check the control panel buttons for stuck buttons or moisture damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door interlock
fixSteps.instruction: Peel back the door seal, remove the retaining ring, and swap the interlock.

whenToCallPro: If both door lock and buttons test fine, the control board may have a faulty relay.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F06 door lock replacement

faq.q: How do I open the door with F06?
faq.a: Wait a few minutes after turning off the machine. If it still will not open, use the emergency release tab behind the filter access panel.

faq.q: What is a door interlock?
faq.a: The electrical device that locks the door and signals the control board that it is safe to start filling with water.

faq.q: Can I bypass the door lock?
faq.a: Never bypass the door interlock. It is a safety device that prevents the door from opening during spin cycles when the drum is rotating at high speed.

title: Heater Relay Fault
shortDescription: The heating element relay on the control board is stuck or malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error F07 indicates the relay controlling the heating element is not functioning correctly. It may be stuck on (continuous heating) or stuck off (no heating). This is safety-critical as uncontrolled heating can damage clothing or the machine.

causes.cause: Heater relay welded shut or open
Wiring fault between heater and board

symptoms: No heating on hot wash cycles

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect for 10 minutes. If F07 returns, continue diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the heater and measure resistance (20-30 ohms). Check insulation to ground (over 2 megaohms).

fixSteps.title: Check the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Test NTC resistance (approximately 20K ohms at room temperature). A faulty NTC can cause relay mismanagement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If heater and NTC are good, the relay has failed. Replace the main PCB.

whenToCallPro: F07 involves safety-critical heating. Professional replacement is recommended if heater and NTC test normal.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F07 heater relay error fix

faq.q: Is F07 dangerous?
faq.a: It can be if the relay is stuck on. If you notice steam or overheating, unplug immediately.

faq.q: Can I wash on cold with F07?
faq.a: Not recommended. If the relay is stuck on, the heater may activate even on cold cycles.

faq.q: What causes relay failure?
faq.a: Wear over time, short-circuited heaters, or power surges can damage relay contacts.

title: Heater Relay Stuck On
shortDescription: The heating element relay is stuck in the closed (on) position.
fullDescription: Error F08 specifically confirms the heater relay contacts are welded together, meaning the heater is permanently powered. This is a safety error that immediately stops the machine to prevent overheating.

causes.cause: Relay contacts welded shut from electrical arcing
Control board PCB damage near relay

symptoms: Heater stays on when it should be off

fixSteps.title: Unplug immediately
fixSteps.instruction: F08 is a safety fault. Unplug right away. Do not run any cycles until repaired.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect heater and measure resistance (20-30 ohms) and insulation to ground (over 2 megaohms).

fixSteps.title: Inspect the relay
fixSteps.instruction: Visually check the relay on the control board for overheating, blackened PCB, or melted plastic.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board and heater if needed
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the main control board. If the heater also tested faulty, replace both.

whenToCallPro: F08 is safety-critical. Professional repair is strongly recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F08 heater relay stuck fix

faq.q: Can F08 cause a fire?
faq.a: Modern machines have multiple safety systems, but a stuck heater relay is a genuine hazard. Unplug immediately.

faq.q: Why does the relay get stuck?
faq.a: Electrical arcing from current surges, especially from a developing heater short circuit, can weld relay contacts together.

faq.q: Should I replace just the relay or the whole board?
faq.a: Full board replacement is the standard and most reliable approach.

title: EEPROM / Software Error
shortDescription: The EEPROM memory on the control board has a data error.
fullDescription: Error F09 indicates the EEPROM chip storing the washer's firmware and settings has corrupted data. The machine cannot operate without valid program data. Usually caused by power interruptions or surges.
symptoms: Machine will not start any cycle

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes. Reconnect and try starting a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Open the top panel and reseat all connectors between the display board and main board.

fixSteps.title: EEPROM reprogramming
fixSteps.instruction: A service technician may reprogram the EEPROM with a diagnostic tool and correct firmware file.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If reprogramming fails, replace with a pre-programmed board for your model.

whenToCallPro: EEPROM faults almost always require professional intervention.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F09 EEPROM error fix

faq.q: What is EEPROM?
faq.a: Non-volatile memory that stores the washer's firmware and settings, retaining data even when powered off.

faq.q: Can a power cut cause F09?
faq.a: Yes, sudden power loss during a write operation can corrupt EEPROM data. Use a surge protector.

faq.q: Is the replacement board pre-programmed?
faq.a: Genuine replacement boards come pre-programmed for specific model ranges.

title: Pressure Switch No Signal
shortDescription: No valid signal from the pressure switch to the control board.
fullDescription: Error F10 means complete communication failure between the pressure switch and control board. Unlike F04 (stuck empty) or F05 (stuck full), F10 indicates no signal at all, often from a disconnected sensor.
symptoms: Fill valve opens but cycle does not progress

fixSteps.title: Check pressure switch connector
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel and ensure the electrical connector on the pressure switch is firmly attached.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check the rubber hose is connected at both ends and not cracked.

fixSteps.title: Test the switch
fixSteps.instruction: With connector removed, check continuity and blow into the hose port to verify the switch changes state.

fixSteps.title: Trace wiring
fixSteps.instruction: If switch is good, trace wiring back to the control board for breaks.

whenToCallPro: If switch, hose, and wiring all test good, the control board has likely failed.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F10 pressure switch error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between F04, F05, and F10?
faq.a: F04 = stuck empty, F05 = stuck full, F10 = no signal at all. F10 is often a disconnected wire.

faq.q: Can vibration cause F10?
faq.a: Yes, excessive vibration can shake connectors loose. Ensure the machine is level.

faq.q: Where is the pressure switch?
faq.a: A round disc-shaped component near the top of the machine, usually on the left side under the top panel.

title: Drain Pump Not Responding
shortDescription: The drain pump is not receiving power or not responding to the control board.
fullDescription: Error F11 means the pump circuit has a fault — the pump may not run at all. Unlike F05 (pump runs but does not drain), F11 is an electrical fault where no power reaches the pump or the pump motor has failed.

fixSteps.title: Check pump connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drain pump and verify the electrical connector is firmly plugged in.

fixSteps.title: Test pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect pump and measure resistance (5-30 ohms). Open circuit means motor failed.

fixSteps.title: Check for voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Reconnect pump and check for mains voltage at connector during drain cycle.

fixSteps.title: Replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If pump tests open circuit, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If pump tests good but no voltage reaches it, the control board needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F11 drain pump no power fix

faq.q: What is the difference between F05 and F11?
faq.a: F05 = pump runs but does not drain (physical blockage). F11 = pump does not run at all (electrical fault).

faq.q: Can I manually drain with F11?
faq.a: Yes, use the drain filter access at the bottom-front of the machine.

faq.q: How long do drain pumps last?
faq.a: Typically 8-12 years with proper filter maintenance.

title: Display / Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication between the display module and main control board has failed.
fullDescription: Error F12 indicates a loss of communication between the display board and the main electronic control board, typically connected by a ribbon cable. The display cannot relay user commands to the main board.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable between boards is loose

fixSteps.title: Reseat board connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Remove top panel and reseat the ribbon cable between display and main board.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect boards and connectors for moisture, corrosion, or detergent residue. Clean with electronics contact cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Test with replacement display
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating fails, try a replacement display board.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: Replace display board or main board as needed. Start with the less expensive display board.

whenToCallPro: If reseating does not work and you are unsure which board is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F12 display communication error fix

faq.q: Can steam cause F12?
faq.a: Yes, steam from overloading or too much detergent can reach the display board, causing communication faults.

faq.q: Which board should I replace first?
faq.a: Start with the interconnect cable, then the display board, then the main board.

faq.q: Is the display board expensive?
faq.a: Display boards typically cost 40-90, much less than the main control board.

title: Dryer Temperature Sensor Fault (Washer-Dryer)
shortDescription: The drying cycle temperature sensor is out of range on washer-dryer models.
fullDescription: Error F13 applies to Indesit washer-dryer combos. The NTC for the drying circuit is open, shorted, or reading outside range. The drying function is disabled until repaired.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry drying.

fixSteps.title: Test dryer NTC
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the dryer NTC near the heating element or air duct. Measure resistance (10K-20K ohms at room temperature).

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect wiring from dryer NTC to control board for heat damage or breaks.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and replace the faulty NTC sensor.

whenToCallPro: If NTC and wiring test fine, the control board dryer circuit may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer dryer F13 temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Does F13 only affect drying?
faq.a: Yes, washing function continues to work normally.

faq.q: Can I still use the washing function?
faq.a: Yes, wash-only cycles should work fine.

faq.q: Is the dryer NTC different from the wash NTC?
faq.a: Yes, they are separate sensors monitoring different circuits.

title: Dryer Heating Element Fault (Washer-Dryer)
shortDescription: The dryer heating element is not working on washer-dryer models.
fullDescription: Error F14 on Indesit washer-dryer units indicates the dryer heater is open circuit or the thermal cut-out has tripped. The drying cycle cannot generate hot air.

fixSteps.title: Check thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes. The thermal fuse may reset after cooling.

fixSteps.title: Test heater element
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance (20-40 ohms). Open circuit means element failed.

fixSteps.title: Test thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Check the thermal cut-out for continuity. If open, it needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace failed component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace heater element or thermal fuse as needed.

whenToCallPro: If heater and fuse test good, the control board dryer relay may have failed.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer dryer F14 heater element fix

faq.q: Why does the thermal fuse trip?
faq.a: Blocked lint filter, restricted airflow, or overloading. Clean the lint filter before each drying cycle.

faq.q: Can I dry without fixing F14?
faq.a: No, only cold air will blow. Washing function still works.

faq.q: Is the dryer heater different from the wash heater?
faq.a: Yes, completely separate components in different locations.

title: Dryer Heater Relay Fault (Washer-Dryer)
shortDescription: The dryer heater relay on the control board is stuck or malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error F15 on Indesit washer-dryer units indicates the relay controlling the dryer heater is stuck closed or open. This is a control board level fault. If stuck on, the dryer overheats; if stuck off, no heat is produced.

fixSteps.title: Safety power off
fixSteps.instruction: If overheating, unplug immediately and let cool completely.

fixSteps.title: Test dryer heater
fixSteps.instruction: Check resistance and insulation to ground. A shorted heater damages the relay.

fixSteps.title: Inspect relay on board
fixSteps.instruction: Check the dryer relay for discoloration, melted plastic, or burnt tracks.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the main board. Also replace heater if it tested faulty.

whenToCallPro: F15 is safety-critical. Professional repair is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer dryer F15 relay error fix

faq.q: Is F15 a fire risk?
faq.a: If the relay is stuck on, yes. Unplug immediately.

faq.q: Can I still use the washer?
faq.a: Using the machine with a known relay fault is not recommended for safety.

faq.q: Can the relay be repaired?
faq.a: Full board replacement is the standard approach.

title: Drum Lock Fault (Top-Loader)
shortDescription: The drum lock or brake mechanism on top-loading models is not functioning.
fullDescription: Error F16 on Hotpoint top-loading models indicates the drum locking mechanism that prevents rotation during loading has failed. The machine cannot safely spin without the drum locked.
symptoms: Drum does not lock in position
Lid cannot open or close properly

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry.

fixSteps.title: Check drum lock mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drum lock assembly and verify the solenoid engages.

fixSteps.title: Test the solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance (30-100 ohms). Open circuit means the coil has burned out.

fixSteps.title: Replace drum lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove mounting screws, disconnect wiring, install new assembly.

whenToCallPro: If drum lock and solenoid test good, the control board may have a faulty output.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit top loader F16 drum lock fix

faq.q: Is F16 only on top-loaders?
faq.a: Primarily yes, it relates to the drum parking brake on top-loading models.

faq.q: Can the drum be manually unlocked?
faq.a: On most models, unplugging releases the drum lock.

faq.q: Why does the drum need to lock?
faq.a: On top-loaders, the drum opening must align with the lid opening for safe loading and unloading.

title: Door Lock Timeout
shortDescription: The door lock did not engage within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error F17 means the door interlock did not confirm it was locked within the timeout period. The lock was commanded but did not respond in time, suggesting a slow or failing mechanism.
symptoms: F17 error shortly after pressing start
Door lock clicks but does not fully engage

fixSteps.title: Ensure door is fully closed
fixSteps.instruction: Push firmly until you hear a solid click. Check nothing is preventing closure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door catch
fixSteps.instruction: Check the striker plate for wear. Replace if worn.

fixSteps.title: Test interlock
fixSteps.instruction: Check voltage at the interlock during start and test feedback contacts for continuity.

fixSteps.title: Replace interlock
fixSteps.instruction: If the interlock is slow to engage, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If interlock and catch are fine, the control board may have a timing issue.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F17 door lock timeout fix

faq.q: What is the difference between F06 and F17?
faq.a: F06 is a general door lock fault. F17 is specifically about the lock not engaging within the timeout.

faq.q: Can low voltage cause F17?
faq.a: Yes, the interlock solenoid may not engage fast enough on low voltage.

faq.q: How long does the machine wait?
faq.a: Typically 15-30 seconds for the interlock to confirm it is locked.

title: Main Processor Communication Error
shortDescription: The main processor on the control board has a critical fault.
fullDescription: Error F18 indicates a serious fault within the main microprocessor. The processor cannot communicate with other board components. This is non-repairable and requires board replacement.

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes. If F18 returns, the board needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure correct mains voltage (230V). Try a different outlet.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the board
fixSteps.instruction: Check for burnt components, swollen capacitors, or discolored PCB areas.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Install a pre-programmed replacement board for your model.

whenToCallPro: F18 is a board-level fault. Professional replacement is the only option if reset fails.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer F18 processor error board replacement

faq.q: Is the machine worth repairing?
faq.a: A board costs 80-200. If the machine is under 8-10 years old and otherwise good, repair is worthwhile.

faq.q: Can F18 be caused by a power outage?
faq.a: Yes, surges or sudden power loss can damage the processor.

faq.q: Do I need the exact board part number?
faq.a: Yes, it must match your model as it comes pre-programmed.

title: Water Supply Problem
shortDescription: The machine is not receiving sufficient water to fill within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error H20 (also displayed as H2O) indicates the washing machine has not filled with enough water within the timeout. This is typically a water supply issue. The fill valves are opening but insufficient water reaches the tub.

causes.cause: Water supply taps turned off or not fully open

symptoms: Fill valve buzzes but no water

fixSteps.title: Check water taps
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure both hot and cold taps are fully open. Turn off and on to confirm flow.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Check for kinks. Disconnect from machine and clean the mesh filter screens.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet valve filters
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mesh filters from inside the valve inlets with needle-nose pliers and clean.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Check for 230V at the valve during fill. If voltage present but no water, valve is faulty.

whenToCallPro: Only if the inlet valve needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Indesit washer H20 water supply error fix

faq.q: Is H20 the same as H2O?
faq.a: Yes, the display represents H2O (water). Same error, different display format.

faq.q: Can low pressure cause H20?
faq.a: Yes, if home water pressure is below about 1 bar, the machine may not fill fast enough.

faq.q: Do I need both hot and cold hoses?
faq.a: Most modern Indesit washers use cold only. Check your manual.
